topic.what: Adaptive ransomware detection combining RL-guided sample reweighting with dual frozen CNN feature extractors
topic.how: Fuses EfficientNetB0 and InceptionV3 features extracted from behavioral JSON-to-image transformed data; Q-learning agent dynamically reweights training samples emphasizing misclassified cases; residual lightweight MLP performs classification
topic.why_matters: Modern ransomware uses polymorphism and obfuscation to evade static models; RL enables adaptive training focus on challenging samples reducing false negatives critical for security

characteristics.what_it_measures: Classification correctness on balanced ransomware vs benign dataset
characteristics.evidence: Stratified 5-fold CV on 500 ransomware 500 benign samples
characteristics.context: Outperforms RF XGBoost SVM LR ANN CNN without RL

characteristics.what_it_measures: Area under ROC curve for binary classification
characteristics.context: Near-perfect separation between ransomware and benign

characteristics.what_it_measures: Positive predictive value for ransomware class
True positive rate for ransomware detection
characteristics.context: Critical for security: minimizes undetected ransomware

characteristics.vs_baseline: 4 FN without RL vs 3 FN with RL

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to train model on dataset
characteristics.context: Frozen backbones eliminate backprop through CNNs
Enables deployment on IoT edge devices

characteristics.vs_baseline: 8 FP without RL vs 6 FP with RL

characteristics.what_it_measures: Harmonic mean of precision and recall

novelty: First integration of RL-based sample weighting with transfer learning for ransomware detection
Q-learning agent with 5 discrete weight actions adaptively reweights training samples based on misclassification feedback
Dual frozen CNN feature fusion (EfficientNetB0 + InceptionV3) with pre-computed global average pooled vectors
Lightweight residual MLP classifier supervised by RL agent vs end-to-end fine-tuning
Dynamic behavioral features transformed to RGB images enabling pretrained CNN feature extraction
open_problems: Adversarial robustness not tested: poisoning and mimicry attacks not validated
Dataset limited to 1000 samples: scalability across diverse ransomware families underexplored
Tabular Q-learning with discrete actions may limit adaptability in complex feature spaces
No defense against adversarial samples engineered to bypass detection
Generalization to unseen ransomware families requires larger evaluation
